title: Read CLI version from package.json at runtime, not hardcoded
context: The CLI's --version flag was hardcoded to 0.1.0, so hunch --version reported 0.1.0 on every release regardless of what npm actually installed. This masked which build users were running and made troubleshooting impossible.
decision: Read the version from package.json dynamically at module load time using an IIFE. Resolve the path relative to the current file location (../../package.json) to handle both dev (src/cli/) and production (dist/cli/) layouts. Fallback to 0.0.0 if the read fails.
consequences: Version now always reflects the actual installed/dev version
Adds file I/O cost at module load (minimal, one-time via IIFE)
Users can now accurately report which build they have
Eliminates sync drift between CLI and npm package.json
Depends on package.json being accessible at runtime in the expected location
alternatives_rejected: Hardcoded literal (was the old approach; caused silent version lies)
Environment variable or CLI argument (requires external tooling changes)
Build-time version injection (requires CI/build system changes)
Querying npm directly (adds dependency and network latency)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Hardcoded literal (was the old approach; caused silent version lies)
Environment variable or CLI argument (requires external tooling changes)
Build-time version injection (requires CI/build system changes)
Querying npm directly (adds dependency and network latency)
